tasks.instruction: Match each description (A-L) to the correct animal. Three descriptions do not match any animal.

options.text: This animal is one of the smartest sea creatures and can communicate using clicks and whistles.
It has amazing eyesight and can spot a small mouse from almost two kilometres away.
It is the largest land animal and can weigh over five thousand kilograms.
It lives in groups called pods and sometimes jumps high out of the water just for fun.
It builds its nest on high cliffs or tall trees to keep its babies safe.
It uses its long trunk to drink water, pick up food, and even greet other members of the herd.
It can swim very fast and uses echolocation to find fish in deep, dark water.
The bald variety is the national symbol of the United States of America.
Baby ones stay close to their mothers for many years and learn which plants are safe to eat.
This animal sleeps upside down and uses sound waves to fly in the dark.
It changes the colour of its skin to hide from enemies.
This insect can carry objects fifty times heavier than its own body.
